data.content:
Planning complete. Implementation can proceed in one knot (no children).

Approach: top-level `autoSync` boolean setting (default false) is picked
up automatically by the existing `backfillMissingSettingsDefaults`
startup hook in `src/instrumentation.ts` — no extra bootstrap needed.

Sync is a module-level singleton in the Next.js server process:
- `src/lib/beats-sync-state.ts` holds the `{ running, projects }`
  snapshot.
- `src/lib/beats-sync-service.ts` owns `getBeatsSyncState()` (used by
  GET) and `triggerBeatsSync()` (used by POST). The trigger captures
  state-before, sets `running = true` synchronously before any `await`
  (so 15 concurrent calls all see the same pre-state and exactly one
  proceeds to launch the job), then `void`-launches the job loop. The
  loop Fisher-Yates shuffles registered repos, calls `kno sync` or
  `bd sync --no-daemon` per repo (cwd = repo path), and sleeps a
  random 2–20 s between repos using injected `delay`/`random` so tests
  stay hermetic.
- `src/app/api/sync/beats/route.ts` wires GET/POST to the service.

Frontend: `src/hooks/use-auto-sync.ts` polls every 180 s when
`settings.autoSync === true`, mounted once via `providers.tsx`. Toggle
lives in `settings-defaults-section.tsx` styled like the existing
terminal-theme switch.

Test coverage: hermetic tests cover AC #1–6 and #9 (15-fold concurrency,
state-before-trigger, GET purity, shuffle + delay, lastSyncedAt update,
schema default + persistence). Known gap: AC #8 (frontend not polling
when disabled) is covered only at the React-Query level via the
settings refetch — `setInterval` is non-hermetic so no default-suite
timer test. Add manual test under `__manual_tests__/` if desired.

Risks: CLI compatibility verified via existing `bd-update.ts` usage of
`bd sync --no-daemon` and `kno sync` (confirmed via `kno sync --help`
during planning). Module-level singleton is safe under Node's single-
threaded event loop; if Foolery ever moves to multi-process serving,
revisit with a file-lock or daemon.
